Feature_Name: Inter-gNB-DU LTM Handover UE Context Setup
Feature_Description: This procedure is used for the case when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation for LTM (LTM: Layered Transmission Mode).
Feature_Objectives: To establish a UE context and set up one or more data bearers in a candidate gNB-DU for a UE undergoing Inter-gNB-DU LTM handover.
To configure CSI resources and LTM-specific information in the candidate gNB-DU for subsequent LTM operation.
Feature_Implementation_Steps: The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration based on L3 measurement reports from the UE.
The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing LTM-specific configuration.

The candidate gNB-DU processes the request and, if accepted, responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 3. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

4. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.

Code_Instructions.description: Data structure defining the format of the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, used by gNB-CU to send configuration to gNB-DU.
Data structure defining the format of the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message, used by gNB-DU to respond to gNB-CU.
Helper function responsible for generating the RRC-specific content and parameters that will be included within the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message.
A function likely involved in the construction or encoding of the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, potentially bridging RRC and F1AP layers.
A generic utility function to copy a sequence of bytes, useful for populating fields within F1AP messages.
A utility function for memory allocation, ensuring that memory is successfully allocated or the program handles the failure gracefully. Essential for dynamic message construction.
A utility function for memory allocation and initialization to zero, ensuring successful allocation or graceful failure. Useful for initializing new data structures for F1AP messages.
A supporting function to activate a Signalling Radio Bearer (SRB), which is a fundamental part of establishing UE context and communication in NR.
A helper function to populate Data Radio Bearer (DRB) setup information, potentially based on an E1AP response, which is crucial for configuring data paths within the UE context setup.
A utility function to retrieve UE-specific data related to the F1AP interface, essential for accessing and managing UE context information on the gNB-CU.

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: Not applicable for the scoped messages in this procedure.
